mnemonics: T-H-R-E-S-H: Think Histogram Regions, Estimate Starting point, Segment Hard edges
keyPoints: Thresholding converts a grayscale image into a binary image based on a threshold value
Global thresholding uses a single threshold value for the entire image, often chosen from the image histogram
The iterative algorithm for finding an optimal global threshold starts with an initial guess (e.g., the mean intensity) and refines it until convergence
commonMistakes: Assuming a single fixed threshold value (e.g., 128) works for all images
Forgetting to convert the image to grayscale before applying thresholding
Misinterpreting the binary output (which pixels are object vs. background)
